name: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (CVN-69)
description: Nimitz-class aircraft carrier leading Carrier Strike Group 2. Deployed to Persian Gulf region since February 2026 in response to escalating tensions. Carries approximately 5,000 personnel and 60+ aircraft including F/A-18E/F Super Hornets.

name: USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78)
description: Ford-class aircraft carrier, the most advanced warship in the US Navy. Deployed to Eastern Mediterranean as part of heightened force posture. Carries approximately 4,500 personnel and 75+ aircraft.

name: USS Kidd (DDG-100)
description: Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer. Part of CSG-2 escort. Equipped with Aegis Combat System and SM-3 missile defense interceptors. Positioned in Strait of Hormuz approach.

name: USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112)
description: Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer. Providing ballistic missile defense coverage in the Gulf of Oman. Equipped with Tomahawk cruise missiles and Aegis BMD capability.

name: USS Ralph Johnson (DDG-114)
description: Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer. Operating in Eastern Mediterranean as part of Ford CSG. Providing air defense and cruise missile strike capability.

name: USS Ohio (SSGN-728)
description: Ohio-class guided-missile submarine. Carries up to 154 Tomahawk cruise missiles. Position approximate — exact location classified. Confirmed deployed to CENTCOM area of operations by Pentagon.

name: USS Iwo Jima (LHD-7)
description: Wasp-class amphibious assault ship. Carries 2,000+ Marines from the 26th Marine Expeditionary Unit. Provides amphibious capability and serves as a helicopter platform for regional operations.

name: INS Magen (Sa'ar 6)
description: Sa'ar 6-class corvette, Israel's most advanced naval vessel. Equipped with Iron Dome naval variant (C-Dome), Barak-8 missiles, and Gabriel V anti-ship missiles. Patrolling Eastern Mediterranean.

name: INS Oz (Sa'ar 6)
description: Sa'ar 6-class corvette providing naval defense and gas platform protection off Israel's coast. Equipped with C-Dome missile defense system for intercepting anti-ship missiles and rockets.

name: INS Dolphin (Dolphin-class)
description: Dolphin-class submarine, one of Israel's most capable naval assets. Position approximate. Widely reported to be capable of launching nuclear-armed cruise missiles, providing Israel's sea-based deterrent.

name: USS Boxer ARG + 11th MEU
description: USS Boxer (LHD-4), USS Portland, USS Comstock with 11th MEU. ~4,000 personnel with F-35Bs. Departed San Diego March 18, transiting to Middle East.
location: Pacific Ocean (en route, departed San Diego)
notes: USS Boxer ARG + 11th MEU (2,500 Marines) departed San Diego March 20. Expected to arrive CENTCOM AOR within 10-14 days. Part of accelerated deployment amid Iran war escalation. Additionally, 82nd Airborne Division deploying to region per Day 26 reports.
position: En route — Day 4 of transit (departed Day 22)
